steps.title: Factor out common terms
steps.content:
Both terms contain factors of 36 and $(x+12)$. Factor them out:
$$36x^{2}(x+12)-36(x+12)^{3} = 36(x+12)[x^{2}-(x+12)^{2}]$$

steps.title: Apply difference of squares
steps.content:
The bracketed expression $x^{2}-(x+12)^{2}$ is a difference of squares. Apply $A^{2}-B^{2}=(A-B)(A+B)$:
$$x^{2}-(x+12)^{2} = [x-(x+12)][x+(x+12)] = (-12)(2x+12)$$
The complete factorization is $36(x+12)(-12)(2x+12)$, so $(2x+12)$ is a factor. That matches choice B — B is correct.
